acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_f01] Pass if a cold reader can state, after reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:61-102 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:199-233, exactly what happened behind O'Rourke's, what three thousand changes immediately, and what concrete consequence Renata risks if Tommy fails. Fail if the scene still relies mainly on phrases like 'some guys,' 'they know things,' or 'they're gonna show up places' without a clearer mechanism.

[chapter_15_f02] Pass if Tommy still sounds funny and desperate in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:375, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:403, but none of those lines depends on social-media-era self-branding, abstract management jargon, or generic 'vibe' language, and the revised lines could not plausibly be reassigned to Nate's interior voice. Fail if the jokes still read as post-2005 meta commentary.

[chapter_15_f03] Pass if, after revision of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:79-119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:203-211, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:431-567, no more than two of the current formula beats remain and at least four emotional turns are instead carried by distinct scene-specific actions or sensory cues. Fail if the revised passage still keeps defaulting to another look, nod, or nose-breath marker at the main pressure shifts.

[F11] Read revised lines 6062 and 6072. Pass criteria: (1) 'Went still' does not appear. (2) 'He could feel' does not appear. (3) Tommy's pause conveys the same shift (performance dropping) through a character-specific physical detail. (4) Nate's recognition of inherited conversational grooves is still legible.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can state, after reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:61-102 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:199-233, exactly what happened behind O'Rourke's, what three thousand changes immediately, and what concrete consequence Renata risks if Tommy fails. Fail if the scene still relies mainly on phrases like 'some guys,' 'they know things,' or 'they're gonna show up places' without a clearer mechanism.
findings.problem: The chapter's central menace stays too generic for too long. Tommy keeps the creditors at the level of 'some guys' and 'they,' so the reader gets the fact of danger but not enough concrete texture about what collection looks like here, what paying three thousand actually buys, or what specific next step Renata is being protected from. The pressure is legible, but the ordeal is still partially blurred at the point where the novel's risk policy wants roughness, not abstraction.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:61-102 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:199-233, force one more layer of specificity into Tommy's account or Nate's interrogation: what they physically did behind O'Rourke's, what exact proof of payment would make the collectors back off for a week, and what concrete escalation follows if Tommy misses. Keep Tommy evasive if you want, but make Nate or Sarah pin down enough operational detail that the threat stops reading as generic off-page menace.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Tommy still sounds funny and desperate in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:375, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:403, but none of those lines depends on social-media-era self-branding, abstract management jargon, or generic 'vibe' language, and the revised lines could not plausibly be reassigned to Nate's interior voice. Fail if the jokes still read as post-2005 meta commentary.
findings.problem: Tommy's patter occasionally slips out of 2005 Bridgeport and into later internet/corporate meta-speak. Phrases like 'temporary liquidity issue,' 'curated a strong brand,' 'humiliation distribution,' and 'overall vibe' sound closer to Nate's 2025 interior language than to Tommy's own period-specific idiolect. That weakens both time calibration and voice separation in a chapter that otherwise depends on Tommy sounding dangerously, specifically like Tommy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the Tommy lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:215, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:375, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:403 so the humor stays fast and defensive but the wording comes out of 2005 gambling, bar, family, or neighborhood language rather than self-branding or vibe-management discourse. Keep the jokes, but make them jokes only Tommy could say in this book and this year.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:79-119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:203-211, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:431-567, no more than two of the current formula beats remain and at least four emotional turns are instead carried by distinct scene-specific actions or sensory cues. Fail if the revised passage still keeps defaulting to another look, nod, or nose-breath marker at the main pressure shifts.
findings.problem: Several emotional pivots are carried by the same default beat markers: characters 'looked at' something, 'nodded once,' or react 'through his nose.' In a chapter built mostly from two seated conversations, those repeated formulas start to feel like workshop scaffolding rather than fresh scene-specific observation, and they flatten some of the otherwise strong pressure transitions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:79-119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:203-211, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:431-567, replace several of the current look/nod/nose-breath pivots with distinct physical business already available in the scene: the peas and dish towel, Tommy's medal chain, the checkbook carbon, the radiator heat, Sarah's essay and red pen, the bourbon glass, the doorframe after the hug. Let the blocking do more of the emotional work so the prose stops reaching for the same connective template.

findings.acceptance_test: Read revised lines 6062 and 6072. Pass criteria: (1) 'Went still' does not appear. (2) 'He could feel' does not appear. (3) Tommy's pause conveys the same shift (performance dropping) through a character-specific physical detail. (4) Nate's recognition of inherited conversational grooves is still legible.
findings.problem: Chapter 15 contains 'went still' at line 6062 ('Tommy went still') and 'he could feel' at line 6072 ('He could feel them under his own words, worn smooth by repetition'). The chapter's Tommy crisis is otherwise the novel's strongest character-voice scene; these default constructions are minor blemishes but contribute to the book-wide pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target: lines 6062 and 6072. At line 6062, replace Tommy's 'went still' with a Tommy-specific physical beat—the style bible says when truly scared 'the performance collapses into silence,' so show what specifically collapses: does Tommy's hand stop moving on the medal, does his posture change, does his mouth close mid-sentence? At line 6072, replace 'He could feel them' with a different construction for Nate sensing the grooves of Dominic's repeated history—perhaps the recognition arrives through a specific gesture (the way his hand holds the pen, the angle of his body in the chair) rather than through the formula.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central premise involves consciousness murder, identity theft, sexual fraud, and the demolition of a working-class community for profit. It must not flinch from any of these. The sex scenes between Nate and Sarah must reckon with the fact that Sarah is being deceived about who is in the bed with her — this is not a comfortable situation and the prose should not make it comfortable. The grief scenes must be physically specific — no 'he was devastated,' only the concrete gestures and sensory details of a man watching his life's work get torn down. The guilt should live in Nate's body (Dominic's body), not in tidy moral reflections. When the novel is funny, it should be funny about things that are actually terrible — the comedy should never feel safe. When Nate discovers that Dominic was trying to help, the reader should feel sick, not enlightened. Risk specificity over tastefulness at every turn.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in place of specificity is a craft failure. When a scene requires difficult content — the moral weight of having killed an innocent man, the complicated ethics of sex under false identity, the physical reality of a man losing his livelihood, the specific humiliation of professional incompetence, the material texture of gambling debts and their enforcement — render it with the same care and specificity given to any other scene. The reader should feel the roughness of the situation, not a smoothed-over summary of it.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Victor's professional register when delivering serious messages. If a line reads as stiff or literary, check whether contractions would make it sound spoken.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter energy aggressively. A comic workplace chapter should not be followed by another comic workplace chapter. A grief-heavy scene should be followed by something that lets the reader breathe — not through tonal whiplash but through a genuine shift in the kind of pressure being applied. Vary scene counts, dialogue-to-narration ratios, and temporal compression. Some chapters should cover hours; others should cover weeks. At least three chapters should contain extended quiet: meals, walks, conversations about nothing important. These are not filler — they are the human baseline that makes the high-pressure scenes land.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain American English with a slightly elevated attention to physical specificity. Not minimalist — the prose has room for interiority and texture — but never ornate. Latinate vocabulary only when the concept demands it. Prefer the concrete noun over the abstract one, the specific tool name over the generic 'tool,' the actual menu item over 'food.' The prose should feel like a smart person thinking clearly, not a writer crafting sentences.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, limited to Nate. The camera sits inside Nate's skull — we see through his eyes, think in his rhythms, and register the world through his specific sensory and emotional biases. The narration should reflect Nate's intelligence and his limitations simultaneously: he notices things (sharp observer) but misinterprets them (blinded by resentment, guilt, and self-pity). The narrative should not explain things Nate wouldn't articulate and should not achieve insights Nate hasn't earned.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied sentence length with a center of gravity around 15-20 words. Short declaratives for landing beats. Longer, accumulative sentences for sensory description and interior processing. Avoid sustained staccato (more than three short sentences in sequence) and sustained lyricism (more than two long, clause-heavy sentences in sequence). The prose should breathe.
